Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SIOBHAN O'BOYLE's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint.

The unopposed motion by specially appearing defendant Siobhan O'Boyle to quash service of summons is granted. O'Boyle challenges service of the summons on her and presents supporting evidence. "When a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction on the ground of improper service of process, the burden is on the plaintiff to prove the existence of jurisdiction by proving, inter alia, the facts requisite to an effective service." (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413 [internal quotation marks, citation, and footnote omitted].) Plaintiff Bank of America N.A. has presented no evidence to carry its burden that effective service was made.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
